finding.summary: Excessive negative stereo correlation (-0.42) and side-dominant energy (side RMS -15 dB vs mid -27 dB) indicate significant out-of-phase content that will cause cancellation in mono, making the track weak or hollow when collapsed.
finding.probable_cause: The track's stereo widening technique or source material uses phase inversion between left and right channels, resulting in a very wide but mono-incompatible image.
finding.confidence_reason: Correlation and mid/side levels are directly measured from an isolated capture with low silence fraction (0.05), so the data is reliable.

evidence_refs.description: Negative stereo correlation indicating out-of-phase content
Mid channel RMS level is much lower than side
Side channel RMS level is 12 dB higher than mid

proposal.reason: The identified problem requires adding a mid/side processor (e.g., JS Mid/Side Encoder or a utility plugin) to reduce side level or adjust polarity, but none of the allowed operations (set_track_volume, set_track_pan, set_fx_param, set_fx_bypass) can insert a new plugin. The current FX chain is empty.
proposal.rejection_reason: Cannot add new FX via the available operations; manual insertion of a mid/side utility is needed.
